top_stories.title: Microsoft Patches Entra ID Role Flaw That Enabled Service Principal Takeover
top_stories.summary: Microsoft patched a flaw in the Microsoft Entra ID "Agent ID Administrator" role that could enable privilege escalation and service principal takeover attacks. Security firm Silverfort discovered that the privileged built-in role, designed to manage AI agent identity lifecycle operations, could be exploited for identity takeover.

top_stories.title: ConsentFix v3 attacks target Azure with automated OAuth abuse
top_stories.summary: A new attack technique called ConsentFix v3 has been identified on hacker forums, targeting Azure environments through automated OAuth abuse. The attack builds on previous OAuth consent phishing methods by adding automation capabilities and increased scaling potential.

top_stories.title: cPanel zero-day exploited for months before patch release (CVE-2026-41940)
top_stories.summary: A critical authentication bypass vulnerability (CVE-2026-41940) in cPanel has been actively exploited in the wild since at least February 23, months before a patch was released. The zero-day affects cPanel, a widely-used web hosting control panel, and allows attackers to bypass authentication mechanisms.

top_stories.title: Cybercrime Groups Using Vishing and SSO Abuse in Rapid SaaS Extortion Attacks
top_stories.summary: Two cybercrime groups, Cordial Spider and Snarky Spider, are conducting rapid SaaS extortion attacks that abuse SSO and use vishing (voice phishing) to gain unauthorized access. The attacks operate almost entirely within SaaS environments, leveraging SSO abuse as a primary vector while leaving minimal forensic traces.

top_stories.title: Learning from the Vercel breach: Shadow AI & OAuth sprawl
top_stories.summary: The Vercel breach demonstrated how a compromised third-party OAuth integration can create widespread access risks across downstream customers. The incident highlights the security risks of OAuth sprawl and shadow AI integrations, where a single compromised app can serve as an entry point into multiple environments.

news.title: Identity is the control plane for distributed infrastructure
news.summary: Teleport CEO Ev Kontsevoy argues that distributed infrastructure across cloud, Kubernetes, databases, and servers requires a unified identity layer rather than layered tools on fragmented identity systems. He advocates for fewer credentials, fewer entry points, and a single identity control plane to provide security and engineering teams with unified visibility and control.

news.title: Poisoned Ruby Gems and Go Modules Exploit CI Pipelines for Credential Theft
news.summary: A software supply chain attack campaign dubbed BufferZoneCorp has been discovered using malicious Ruby gems and Go modules to steal credentials and tamper with GitHub Actions CI pipelines. The attack involves sleeper packages that deliver payloads enabling credential theft and SSH persistence.

news.title: IAM Roles Anywhere now enforces VPC endpoint policies for the CreateSession API
news.summary: AWS IAM Roles Anywhere now supports VPC endpoint policies for the CreateSession API, allowing administrators to control access at the network level. This update enables organizations to configure allow or deny rules on the CreateSession API through VPC endpoint policies.

news.title: IAM tools help Oracle Red Bull Racing keep pace with strict F1 regulations
news.summary: Oracle Red Bull Racing implemented 1Password's identity technology to improve efficiency in aerodynamics testing procedures while complying with strict F1 regulations. The deployment addressed access management needs within the racing team's technical operations.

news.title: Nine-year-old Linux kernel flaw enables reliable local privilege escalation (CVE-2026-31431)
news.summary: Researchers at Theori disclosed a high-severity local privilege escalation vulnerability (CVE-2026-31431) in the Linux kernel, affecting virtually all major Linux distributions shipped since 2017. A working proof-of-concept exploit is publicly available, and the flaw enables reliable privilege escalation from local access, directly relevant to PAM and least-privilege access controls.

news.title: Govern your bots carefully or chaos could ensue
news.summary: Gartner warns that Global Fortune 500 enterprises are expected to run more than 150,000 AI agents by 2028, up from fewer than 15 today. Without proper governance frameworks, the proliferation of AI agents poses identity and access management challenges related to non-human identities and authorization sprawl.

news.title: SAP-Related npm Packages Compromised in Credential-Stealing Supply Chain Attack
news.summary: A supply chain attack campaign dubbed "Mini Shai-Hulud" has compromised SAP-related npm packages with credential-stealing malware. The attack targets credentials through malicious npm packages associated with SAP, as reported by multiple security firms including Aikido Security, Onapsis, and Wiz.

news.title: Webinar: How to Automate Exposure Validation to Match the Speed of AI Attacks
news.summary: A webinar is being promoted around the threat of AI-automated attacks targeting Active Directory and Domain Admin credentials. The session focuses on autonomous agents capable of mapping Active Directory and escalating privileges rapidly, addressing defensive workflows for identity and access-related infrastructure.

news.title: Critical cPanel Authentication Vulnerability Identified — Update Your Server Immediately
news.summary: cPanel has released security updates to fix a critical authentication vulnerability affecting all supported versions of cPanel and WebHost Manager (WHM). The flaw impacts multiple authentication paths and could allow attackers to gain unauthorized access to the control panel software.

news.title: Organizing Agents’ memory at scale: Namespace design patterns in AgentCore Memory
news.summary: AWS published a technical post on AgentCore Memory covering namespace design patterns and IAM-based access control implementation. The IAM component is a supporting feature within a broader AI agent memory architecture discussion rather than a standalone IAM topic.

news.title: AWS Transfer Family Terraform module now supports Okta and Microsoft Entra ID integration examples
news.summary: AWS has updated its Transfer Family Terraform module to include end-to-end deployment examples integrating Okta and Microsoft Entra ID as custom identity providers. The update enables enterprises to use these IdPs for authentication and access control on AWS Transfer Family endpoints.

news.title: Why Secure Data Movement Is the Zero Trust Bottleneck Nobody Talks About
news.summary: New research from a security team surveyed 500 security professionals and identified secure data movement as a significant bottleneck in Zero Trust program implementation. The article argues that connectivity assumptions undermine Zero Trust architectures, with findings published in the Cyber360: Defending the Digital Battlespace report.

news.title: Access control with IAM Identity Center session tags
news.summary: AWS IAM Identity Center session tags can be used to implement access control across multiple accounts in an organization's AWS environment. The article details how session tags enable centralized, scalable workforce access management within AWS IAM Identity Center.

news.title: Why AI agents are triggering a rethink of enterprise identity
news.summary: Computer Weekly's Security Think Tank examines how agentic AI is affecting enterprise identity and access management practices. The article focuses on the specific security stack impacts of AI agents operating autonomously within IAM frameworks.

news.title: Silverfort acquires Fabrix Security to bring AI decision-making to runtime access control
news.summary: Silverfort has acquired AI-native identity security company Fabrix Security for an undisclosed sum. Fabrix, founded in 2024, provides AI-driven capabilities that Silverfort aims to integrate into runtime access control decision-making within its unified identity security platform.

news.title: ‘Scattered Spider’ Member ‘Tylerb’ Pleads Guilty
news.summary: Tyler Robert Buchanan, a 24-year-old British national and senior member of Scattered Spider, pleaded guilty to wire fraud conspiracy and aggravated identity theft. Buchanan admitted involvement in SMS phishing attacks in summer 2022 that compromised at least a dozen major technology companies and resulted in theft of tens of millions of dollars in cryptocurrency.

news.title: Russia Hacked Routers to Steal Microsoft Office Tokens
news.summary: Russian military intelligence hackers exploited vulnerabilities in older internet routers to harvest Microsoft Office authentication tokens from over 18,000 networks. The campaign extracted authentication tokens without deploying malware, enabling silent credential theft at scale.
